Topic (Instruction Focus): Sub Day / BPA State Competition — Mr. McMaster + 3 students in Columbus. Remaining students follow this priority list in order: 1) Finish Web Design Team Walkthrough lessons (due today). 2) If walkthroughs are complete → begin the AI-Powered Data Dashboard assignment (cars2026 fuel economy data — see Google Classroom). 3) If dashboard is complete → ensure Workplace Success module is finished. 4) If everything above is done → continue the long Blender tutorial (4-hour version). Students should be self-directed and follow the priority list in order.
Daily Objectives (Learner Will Be Able T: 1. Collaborate with team members to finalize a markdown walkthrough covering assigned web design topics (Bootstrap, Forms, SEO/Accessibility, or Troubleshooting). 2. Apply writing process techniques to check walkthrough content for accuracy and completeness. 3. Write effective prompts to Gemini AI to generate a data-driven HTML/JS dashboard using the cars2026.json dataset. 4. Use fetch() to load external JSON data into a web page and interpret AI-generated JavaScript. 5. Demonstrate self-direction by progressing through the priority list when primary tasks are complete.
State Competencies (Standards): 1.2.2: Deliver formal and informal presentations. 1.2.5: Communicate information for an intended audience and purpose. 1.1.7: Apply problem-solving and critical-thinking skills. 1.1.9: Give and receive constructive feedback. 6.5.13: Integrate responsive design (Team 1 — Bootstrap). 6.4.1: Design a data entry form from specifications (Team 2 — Forms). 6.5.14: Incorporate SEO into webpages (Team 3 — SEO). 6.1.2: Plan a web page considering ADA requirements (Team 3 — Accessibility). 2.11.2: Select troubleshooting methodology (Team 4). 2.12.3: Develop test cases (Team 4). 6.1.4: Use writing process to check web page for format and text accuracy. 6.3.1: Select and apply scripting languages used in web development (AI Dashboard). 6.3.2: Insert client-side script into a web page (AI Dashboard). 6.5.3: Utilize standard web programming languages in website development (AI Dashboard). 6.4.7: Code scripting to interact with data sources (AI Dashboard). 2.7.2: Describe ways to present data (AI Dashboard).
Assessment / Grade Entry: Formative: Check walkthrough markdown progress for completeness and code accuracy. AI Dashboard: dashboard_v1.html showing data loaded with summary stats and chart. Monitor sub report for student engagement and task completion.

Topic (Instruction Focus): Sub Day / BPA State Competition (Day 2) — Mr. McMaster + 3 students still in Columbus. Remaining students continue the same priority list: 1) Finalize walkthroughs and practice presenting for Wednesday. 2) Continue or begin the AI-Powered Data Dashboard — add interactive features (Part 2) and iterate with Gemini. 3) Ensure Workplace Success is complete. 4) Blender tutorial. Teams should rehearse their presentations for Wednesday.
Daily Objectives (Learner Will Be Able T: 1. Rehearse team walkthrough presentations and verify all code examples are copy-pasteable and working. 2. Apply peer review techniques to refine walkthrough content before presenting. 3. Iterate on AI-generated dashboard code by prompting Gemini to add interactive features (filters, charts, comparisons). 4. Save versioned HTML files (dashboard_v2, v3, etc.) to track iterations. 5. Continue progressing through Workplace Success or Blender tutorial if primary tasks are complete.
State Competencies (Standards): 1.2.2: Deliver formal and informal presentations. 1.2.5: Communicate information for an intended audience. 1.2.7: Use problem-solving and consensus-building techniques. 1.1.9: Give and receive constructive feedback. 6.1.4: Use writing process to check format and text accuracy. 6.5.10: Develop and execute usability tests on a completed website. 2.11.6: Test a solution. 2.12.3: Develop test cases. 6.3.1: Select and apply scripting languages used in web development (AI Dashboard). 6.5.3: Utilize standard web programming languages (AI Dashboard). 6.5.8: Format website layout (AI Dashboard). 6.4.7: Code scripting to interact with data sources (AI Dashboard). 2.7.2: Describe ways to present data (AI Dashboard). 1.1.7: Apply problem-solving and critical-thinking skills (AI Dashboard).
Assessment / Grade Entry: Formative: Sub report on student participation. Teams should have finalized walkthrough docs ready for Wednesday. AI Dashboard: Best two dashboard_vX.html files and reflection paragraph submitted via Google Classroom.

Topic (Instruction Focus): Web Design Exam Prep: Team Walkthrough Presentations — Teams present their markdown walkthroughs live from Mr. McMaster's computer (~20-25 min each). Class follows along and builds working code on their own machines. Topics: Team 1 (Bootstrap Basics) | Team 2 (HTML Forms & Validation) | Team 3 (SEO / Accessibility / Deployment) | Team 4 (Troubleshooting / QA / Dev Tools).
Daily Objectives (Learner Will Be Able T: 1. Build responsive layouts using Bootstrap grid and components. 2. Design and code HTML forms with various input types and validation. 3. Apply SEO best practices and ADA accessibility quick wins to a web page. 4. Use Chrome DevTools to identify and fix HTML/CSS bugs. 5. Write and execute simple test cases for a form page.
State Competencies (Standards): 1.2.2: Deliver formal and informal presentations. 1.2.5: Communicate information for an intended audience and purpose. 6.5.13: Integrate responsive design into web development. 6.1.7: Integrate styles (inline or external CSS). 6.5.8: Format website layout. 6.4.1: Design a data entry form from specifications. 6.4.2: Write HTML code to add a form to a web page. 6.4.3: Write HTML code to add text entry fields and other user inputs. 6.4.5: Write HTML code to add a working button to a form. 6.5.14: Incorporate SEO into webpages. 6.1.2: Plan a web page considering ADA requirements. 6.5.12: Publish the completed website to a web server (GitHub Pages). 2.11.1: Identify the problem. 2.11.2: Select troubleshooting methodology. 2.12.3: Develop test cases.
Assessment / Grade Entry: Formative: Follow-along code check — verify students have working code from each team's walkthrough. Summative: Team Presentation rubric (content accuracy, code quality, delivery, audience engagement).

Topic (Instruction Focus): AI-Assisted Data Dashboards (Day 1 — Intro) — Students are introduced to using Gemini AI to generate interactive data dashboards. Teacher demo: prompting Gemini to transform Titanic CSV data into a single-page HTML/CSS/JS dashboard with charts and filtering. Students set up GitHub Codespaces, fork a starter repo, and use Gemini prompts to build their own Titanic data dashboard. Data is stored as a JSON file in the repo. Focus: writing effective AI prompts, understanding the generated code, and iterating on output.
Daily Objectives (Learner Will Be Able T: 1. Set up and use GitHub Codespaces as a cloud-based development environment. 2. Write effective prompts to an AI tool (Gemini) to generate a functional HTML/JS data dashboard. 3. Store and reference a JSON dataset within a GitHub repository. 4. Read and interpret AI-generated JavaScript that renders charts and interactive elements. 5. Iterate on AI output by refining prompts to improve dashboard functionality.
State Competencies (Standards): 6.3.1: Select and apply scripting languages used in web development. 6.3.2: Insert client-side script into a web page. 6.5.5: Select an integrated development environment (IDE). 6.5.3: Utilize standard web programming languages in website development. 6.5.8: Format website layout. 6.4.7: Code scripting to interact with data sources. 2.7.2: Describe ways to present data (responsive web design, web applications). 2.7.8: Describe the difference between static and dynamic sites. 1.1.7: Apply problem-solving and critical-thinking skills. 6.1.4: Use writing process techniques to check the web page for format and text accuracy.
Assessment / Grade Entry: Formative: Working Titanic dashboard deployed via Live Server in Codespaces. Screenshot or live URL check showing at least one chart and one interactive filter.

Topic (Instruction Focus): AI-Assisted Data Dashboards (Day 2 — Level Up with React) — Students use Gemini to build a more dynamic React-based dashboard using NBA player/team data. They prompt Gemini to scaffold a React app with components for charts, tables, and filters. Data is stored as a JSON file in the repo. Students deploy their finished dashboard to GitHub Pages directly from Codespaces. End of class: gallery walk to see each other's dashboards.
Daily Objectives (Learner Will Be Able T: 1. Use AI prompts to generate a React-based single-page application with data visualization components. 2. Deploy a React project to GitHub Pages from GitHub Codespaces. 3. Integrate a JSON dataset into a React application using props and state. 4. Evaluate and debug AI-generated React code for correctness and usability. 5. Present a working deployed dashboard via a live GitHub Pages URL.
State Competencies (Standards): 6.3.1: Select and apply scripting languages used in web development. 6.5.3: Utilize standard web programming languages in website development. 6.5.12: Publish the completed website to a web server (GitHub Pages). 6.5.13: Integrate responsive design into web development. 6.4.7: Code scripting to interact with data sources. 2.7.2: Describe ways to present data. 6.5.10: Develop and execute usability tests on a completed website. 6.1.2: Plan a web page considering subject, devices, audience, and layout. 1.1.7: Apply problem-solving and critical-thinking skills. 2.11.6: Test a solution.
Assessment / Grade Entry: Formative: Working NBA React dashboard deployed to GitHub Pages — live URL submitted. Summative: AI Dashboard Project rubric (prompt quality, code comprehension, data integration, deployment, design). Weekly Participation Points (25pts).

Date: Teacher Note: Mon/Tue sub plans align with the Sprint Walkthrough Assignment. AI Dashboard assignment uses 2026 EPA fuel economy data (cars2026.json). AI was used to help draft portions of this lesson plan. Competencies mapped from Ohio 145010 Web Design standards.
